Photo by Miha Meglic. Replaced the earlier overview hero selection on 2026-04-09 to make the first impression read as a broader industrial automation control-center overview rather than a single-service scene.
Photo by Mika Baumeister. Selected for the SCADA overview card to keep the thumbnail centered on interface clarity.
Photo by Fernando Narvaez. Selected for the SCADA detail hero to add an operations-led first impression.
Photo by ThisisEngineering. Selected for the SCADA detail feature block to reinforce operator visibility and interface context.
Photo by Mick Haupt. Selected for the I&E overview card to keep the thumbnail tied to instrumentation and panel recognition.
Photo by Alireza Hatami. Selected for the I&E detail hero to foreground field-readiness and on-site execution.
Photo by iSawRed. Selected for the I&E detail feature block to emphasize field instrumentation and electrical-panel context.
Photo by Aleksandr Lyaptsev. Selected for the PLC and DCS overview card to keep cabinet-level automation hardware readable.
Photo by TECNIC Bioprocess Solutions. Selected for the PLC and DCS detail hero to emphasize staged programming and migration work.
Photo by ThisisEngineering. Selected for the PLC and DCS detail feature block to support programming, migration, and staged cutover narratives.
Photo by Tyler. Selected for the PI Server overview card to make data infrastructure legible at thumbnail size.
Photo by Brett Sayles. Selected for the PI Server detail hero to communicate an operations-grade data backbone.
Photo by Tyler. Selected for the PI Server detail feature block to convey plant-data infrastructure and dashboard operations.
Photo by panumas nikhomkhai. Selected for the Historians overview card to keep the thumbnail focused on retention infrastructure.
Photo by Battlecreek Coffee Roasters. Selected for the Historians detail hero to introduce operator review and data continuity context.
Photo by Tyler. Selected for the Historians detail feature block to reinforce long-term data retention and rack-level infrastructure.
Photo by Jordan Harrison. Selected for the Network Design overview card to keep the thumbnail aligned with OT cabling and topology signals.
Photo by Albert Stoynov. Selected for the Network Design detail hero to signal resilient physical OT network infrastructure.
Photo by Fabio Sasso. Selected for the Network Design detail feature block to express topology, connectivity, and maintainable OT networking.
Photo by Debbie Whittam. Selected for the Remote Monitoring overview card to make multi-site visibility clear in compact form.
Photo by Sergey Sergeev. Selected for the Remote Monitoring detail hero to foreground distributed operations and rapid data access.
Photo by Tasha Kostyuk. Selected for the Remote Monitoring and Data detail feature block to emphasize remote visibility and multi-screen monitoring.
Photo by Joao Jesus. Selected for the Alarm Monitoring overview card to keep the thumbnail tied to recognizable alarm hardware.
Photo by Sergey Sergeev. Selected for the Alarm Monitoring detail hero to establish response speed and operator awareness.
Photo by Nopparuj Lamaikul. Selected for the Alarm Monitoring detail feature block to reinforce alert visibility and operator response context.
